_comment: REAL records only, pre-verified (reviewed:true). San Francisco rows are transcribed from SF DataSF LIVES (pyih-qa8i) — the city's open inspection feed, frozen at its last 2019 pull (no current public successor; verified 2026-06); the true 2019 dates make the staleness visible. DC/Newark/Bergen/Florida rows are documented data-availability gaps (not_public_online): portals are JS/CAPTCHA/robots-gated or OPRA-only, so inspection RESULTS are null — the gap is encoded truthfully (never 'no inspection', never fabricated).
englishViolationSummary: Improper storage of equipment, utensils or linens (low risk).
sourceUrlOrDocRef: SF DataSF LIVES (pyih-qa8i): STARBUCKS COFFEE #531, inspected 2019-09-25

englishViolationSummary: Unclean or degraded floors, walls or ceilings (low risk).
sourceUrlOrDocRef: SF DataSF LIVES (pyih-qa8i): STARBUCKS COFFEE CO, 1231 Market St, inspected 2019-09-10

regulatoryAgency: DC Health — Food Safety & Hygiene Inspection Services
sourceUrlOrDocRef: https://dc.healthinspections.us/ (per-establishment pass/fail search; JS app, no open dataset/API)
recommendedAction: DC publishes per-establishment reports via HealthSpace (JS search, no bulk API) — manual per-record lookup required. Do NOT record as 'no inspection'.

regulatoryAgency: Newark Dept. of Health & Community Wellness — Food & Drug Bureau
sourceUrlOrDocRef: Newark Health Inspection Lookup (JS/CAPTCHA-gated) / OPRA via the Food & Drug Bureau
recommendedAction: Online lookup is JS/CAPTCHA-gated — do NOT bypass bot-detection; submit an OPRA request. Never record as 'no inspection'.

regulatoryAgency: Bergen County Dept. of Health Services / municipal health depts
storeName: STARBUCKS (Bergen County, NJ — brand-level coverage marker)
sourceUrlOrDocRef: No countywide online DB (confirmed) — OPRA request per municipality
recommendedAction: No countywide online database — OPRA per municipality (~10 business days). Never record as 'no inspection'.

regulatoryAgency: Florida Dept. of Agriculture & Consumer Services (FDACS) — Food Permit Center
recommendedAction: FDACS robots.txt is default-deny on /search — do NOT scrape; use the UI manually or a public-records request. Never record as 'no inspection'.
